Title: Powdery mildew induces chloroplast storage lipid formation at the expense of host thylakoids to promote spore production

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: triacylglycerol (TAG) related lipid flux affecting powdery mildew spore production
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: null mutant analysis (abca9-1; abca9-3); powdery mildew spore counting
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ABCA9 increases powdery mildew spore production, indicating ER fatty acid/acyl-CoA import for canonical ER TAG synthesis hinders fungal reproduction rather than supporting it.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABCA9, an ER FA/acyl-CoA importer linked to seed TAG accumulation, was tested genetically in Arabidopsis. 2. abca9 null mutants showed enhanced powdery mildew spore production versus WT. 3. Therefore canonical ER-directed acyl import does not support the infection-induced beneficial TAG pool, and instead opposes the chloroplast TAG pathway benefiting the fungus.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Powdery mildew infection by Golovinomyces orontii MGH1; spore production assayed at 9 dpi
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage lipid relevant to pathogen nutrition/development
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing ER acyl import/TAG formation may redirect lipid precursors toward chloroplast TAG synthesis or alter pathogen outcomes, but likely undesirable if seed oil is targeted.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: abca9 reduces seed TAG in prior literature; here increased pathogen spore production suggests reduced resistance outcome.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DGAT3 class; soluble metalloprotein with thioredoxin-like ferredoxin domain and [2Fe-2S] cluster; chloroplast transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for powdery mildew-induced chloroplast TAG synthesis; canonical rate-limiting designation not directly established for all contexts
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: diacylglycerol plus acyl-CoA, preferentially C18:3 and C18:2 acyl substrates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: triacylglycerol, especially species enriched in 18:3/18:2-containing TAG 54:9, 54:8, 54:7 and likely TAG 50:6
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to form triacylglycerol, preferentially incorporating thylakoid-derived 18:3 and 18:2 acyl chains in chloroplasts.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: infected leaf mesophyll chloroplasts underlying haustoria; chloroplast lipid bodies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed; highest reported in hypocotyl and mature/senescent leaf petioles and stems; not strongly induced by pathogen or abiotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mature leaves; expression not strongly induced by powdery mildew, but function evident during infection
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter used for transient AtDGAT3-GFP localization in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: spray-induced gene silencing; T-DNA null mutant dgat3-2; CRISPR mutants dgat3-3.1/dgat3-3.2; transient GFP localization; chloroplast isolation; TLC; direct infusion MS of chloroplast TAGs; powdery mildew spore counts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes powdery mildew-induced chloroplast TAG accumulation and fungal spore production; dgat3 loss reduces chloroplast TAGs by ~60% and lowers spore production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGAT3 is chloroplast localized, unlike canonical ER DGAT1/DGAT2. 2. DGAT3 silencing or knockout reduces powdery mildew spore production without obvious developmental defects. 3. dgat3 mutants show ~60% reduction in infection-induced chloroplast TAG and strong decreases in TAG species enriched in 18:3/18:2 thylakoid-derived acyl chains. 4. DGAT3 therefore mediates plastid TAG synthesis from thylakoid-derived substrates during infection.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Powdery mildew infection by Golovinomyces orontii MGH1; chloroplast TAG assessed at 12 dpi; localization tested by Agrobacterium transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense lipid likely supporting fungal asexual reproduction and/or providing lipid precursors/signals; potentially useful nutritionally as C18:3/C18:2-rich oil
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to engineer leaf oil production enriched in nutritionally desirable C18:3/C18:2; may be stacked with strategies increasing precursor flux and possibly reducing DGAT1 competition; must manage thylakoid degradation and control of DGAT3 activity/stability.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely linked to thylakoid breakdown and chloroplast remodeling; could compromise photosynthetic membranes if strongly activated. No obvious developmental phenotype in mutants, but overproduction strategy may reduce photosynthetic function.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS lipidomics; direct infusion MS/MS chloroplast TAG profiling; MassIVE accession MSV000093317
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer remaining ~40% plastid TAG may also involve PES1/PES2; DGAT3 activity may depend more on substrate availability and redox/[2Fe-2S] state than transcriptional induction.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Reported in paper as catalyzing final and rate-limiting step in canonical ER TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: diacylglycerol plus acyl-CoA; prefers C16:0 according to cited literature
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to produce triacylglycerol in the ER.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product-supporting chloroplast TAG branch; forms alternative TAG pool
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: null mutants dgat1-1 and dgat1-2; powdery mildew spore counting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of DGAT1 enhances powdery mildew spore production, indicating ER TAG synthesis opposes the chloroplast TAG pathway that supports fungal reproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGAT1 was tested as a canonical ER TAG synthesis enzyme. 2. Independent dgat1 mutants showed increased powdery mildew spore production. 3. Thus ER TAG synthesis via DGAT1 does not support, and instead hinders, fungal asexual reproduction likely by diverting precursors away from chloroplast TAG formation and/or generating defensive lipid droplets.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Powdery mildew infection by Golovinomyces orontii MGH1; spore production assayed at 9 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipid; in this context, ER-derived TAG/LD formation may restrict pathogen access or support defense-associated chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing DGAT1 may help redirect flux to chloroplast DGAT3-derived nutritionally desirable leaf oils, but may increase susceptibility to powdery mildew and reduce seed oil based on prior studies.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Known dominant role in seed oil accumulation; loss may reduce seed TAG and increase pathogen reproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Opposing phenotype to DGAT3 underscores distinct functional TAG pools in chloroplast versus ER.
